free_text: Excitation functions of longer lived radionuclides
formed by deuteron irradiation of germanium
Natural germanium vacuum evaporated onto kapton
backing (25um). Thickness of the evaporated layer was
determined by measuring the weight. Typical thickness
was 2 um. 26 kapton-Ge-Ge-kapton sandwich targets were
created by grouping the lowest and highest mass foils
with Ge layers face to face. Al and Ti foils were
inserted in the stack for monitoring the beam.
Irradiation was
done in a Faraday-cup type target holder with 50 MeV
deuteron beam of 50nA for 1 hour. Beam intensity was
monitored by using monitor reactions. Activity was
measured with no chemical separation. Several series
of spectra were recorded to be able to follow the
decay. Detector dead time was kept below 7%. Decay
data were taken from NUDAT online data library.
, Al+d
,  Ti+d
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-1.9MeV in the last
foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included.
surface density of target nuclei(4%),
incident charged particle flux (6%),
nuclear decay and branching data(3%)
detector efficiency (4-9%)
statistical and peak fitting (2-8%)
peak area after separation of complex
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL-2013.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
Entry was checked by the corresponding author.
